extra.message: User input from params['id'] is concatenated directly into SQL query without parameterization. This allows SQL injection attacks to extract, modify, or delete database records.
User input from params['comment'] is concatenated directly into SQL INSERT statement without parameterization. Combined with time.ctime() in the same query, this allows SQL injection.
User input from params.get('password', '') is concatenated directly into SQL query. While username is sanitized with regex, password field has no protection and allows SQL injection attacks.
User input from params['object'] is passed directly to pickle.loads() without validation. This allows remote code execution through deserialization attacks.
User input from params['path'] is used with os.path.abspath() which does not prevent directory traversal attacks. An attacker can read arbitrary files on the system using '../' sequences.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory

extra.message: User input from params['domain'] is passed directly to subprocess.run() with shell=True. This allows command injection attacks to execute arbitrary OS commands.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command

extra.message: XML parser is configured with load_dtd=True, resolve_entities=True, and no_network=False. Combined with user-controlled XML input in params['xml'], this enables XXE attacks to read files and perform SSRF.

cwe: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

extra.message: User input from params['name'] is interpolated directly into XPath expression without escaping. This allows XPath injection to bypass access controls and extract sensitive data.

cwe: CWE-643: Improper Neutralization of Data within XPath Expressions

extra.message: User-controlled code from params['include'] (which can be a remote URL) is executed directly using exec(). This allows arbitrary code execution (Remote Code Execution).

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code

extra.message: User input from params['v'] is inserted into HTML via regex replacement without proper escaping. This allows Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation

extra.message: User input from params['callback'] is used in JSONP response without sanitization. This enables DOM-based XSS attacks.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation

extra.message: User input from params['redir'] is used directly in HTTP meta refresh without validation. This allows open redirect attacks to redirect users to malicious sites.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site

extra.message: User input from params.get('charset', 'utf8') is inserted directly into HTTP Content-Type header without validation. This allows HTTP header injection attacks.

cwe: CWE-113: Improper Neutralization of CRLF Sequences in HTTP Headers

extra.message: User input from params['size'] is used directly to create large strings without validation or limits. This allows Denial of Service attacks through memory exhaustion.
User input from params['path'] is used with urllib.request.urlopen() without validation. This allows Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) attacks to access internal resources and services.
